questions.question: What is matter composed of?
questions.explanation: Experiments such as grinding chalk and dissolving sugar show that matter is made of tiny particles that cannot be seen with the naked eye.

questions.question: What are constituent particles?
questions.correct_answer: The smallest units that make up a substance
questions.explanation: A substance like chalk or sugar consists of many tiny particles called constituent particles.

questions.question: What are interparticle spaces?
questions.explanation: When sugar dissolves in water, sugar particles occupy spaces between water particles.

questions.question: What are interparticle attractions?
questions.correct_answer: Forces of attraction between particles of matter
questions.explanation: These forces hold particles together and determine the state of matter.

questions.question: What are the properties of solids?
questions.correct_answer: Solids have a definite shape and definite volume
questions.explanation: Particles in solids are tightly packed and held by strong attractive forces.

questions.question: Why do liquids take the shape of their container?
questions.correct_answer: Because liquid particles can move freely within a limited space
questions.explanation: Particles in liquids are less tightly packed than solids, allowing them to flow and take the container's shape.

questions.question: Why do gases fill the entire available space?
questions.correct_answer: Because gas particles move freely in all directions
questions.explanation: Gas particles have very weak attractive forces and large spaces between them.

questions.question: Why can gases be compressed easily?
questions.correct_answer: Because there are large spaces between gas particles
questions.explanation: Applying pressure pushes the particles closer together.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
questions.correct_answer: The spreading of particles from one place to another due to their motion
questions.explanation: Particles move constantly, causing substances to spread throughout a liquid or gas.

questions.question: Why does the smell of perfume spread throughout a room?
questions.correct_answer: Because gas particles move continuously and diffuse through air
questions.explanation: Air particles collide with fragrance particles and help them spread across the room.

notes.title: Particle Nature of Matter
notes.content: Matter is composed of extremely small particles that cannot be seen with the naked eye.

notes.title: Interparticle Attraction
notes.content: Particles of matter attract each other. The strength of this attraction determines the state of matter.

notes.title: States of Matter
notes.content: Matter exists in three main states: solid, liquid, and gas, each with different particle arrangements and movements.

notes.title: Interparticle Spacing
notes.content: The space between particles is smallest in solids, larger in liquids, and largest in gases.

notes.title: Movement of Particles
notes.content: Particles in matter are always moving. Their movement increases when temperature increases.

facts.fact: Matter is made up of extremely small particles called atoms and molecules.
Interparticle attraction is strongest in solids and weakest in gases.
Particles in liquids move more freely than particles in solids.
Diffusion occurs faster in gases than in liquids.
